items.original_candidate: Ed Laredixon (likely from "Ed Lara Dixon" or similar Black-United-Youth member)
items.evidence: The transcript context is a near-perfect biographical match for Ed Whitfield (b. 1949, Little Rock, AR; Cornell '70). Speaker says: "those young men coming out of Cornell University with the gun, strapped around them ... one of those young men was from Little Rock, Arkansas ... I think he lives in Chapel Hill ... it was Ed Laredixon. And Eric's somebody ... it was the three young men that was on the front of that paper ... when they left, Ed Laredixon came to North Carolina to further his education and actually got married and lived there for several years." Ed Whitfield was a leader of the April 19, 1969 Willard Straight Hall takeover, was featured on the May 5, 1969 Newsweek cover ("Universities Under the Gun: Militants at Cornell") alongside Eric Evans (the speaker's "Eric's somebody"), was born/raised in Little Rock, and moved to North Carolina after Cornell to teach at Malcolm X Liberation University in Greensboro. Whisper mis-rendered "Whitfield" as "Laredixon"/"Larry Dixon" twice (positions 60095, 60398, 71246 in corrected .txt).
